Undergrad scenario body: You are walking to an important meeting with your professor. Suddenly your mind goes blank as you reach for the doorknob to her office. You think about your mind going blank and think that this is normal.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is it scary that your mind went blank right before your meeting?

Undergrad scenario body: You are about to give an important presentation. You are anxious about it. This feeling makes you exhilarated.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you excited about the presentation?

Undergrad scenario body: You are at a CIO meeting and find out that an opportunity for promotion in your organization just came up. You think you might be eligible and decide to ask your friend in exec for more details. They tell you that the selection criteria are very strict and that your application will likely be rejected.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: The next day, you bump into the exec member in the corridor. You then realize that although they think you may not be ready for promotion just yet, they strongly believe you are capable of being ready for one next year and are likely to succeed.
Undergrad comprehension question: The exec member thinks you shouldn't apply for a promotion now because ...
Undergrad correct response: You will be ready for one next year.

Undergrad scenario body: It is your first day at a new job, and you report to your manager. As you approach them, you stumble, then recover. They noticed, and their first impression of you is probably that you are very prepared.
Undergrad comprehension question: Your manager will probably think that ...
Undergrad correct response: You are ready to take on challenges.

Undergrad scenario body: Your new teaching assistant calls a meeting to meet everyone and asks each of you in turn to present yourself and your prior experience. When it is your turn to speak, you pause frequently. As you reflect on the meeting later in the day, you feel that the teaching assistant thought you sounded silly.
Undergrad comprehension question: Your teaching assistant probably found you to be ...

Undergrad scenario body: Your neighbors were really loud last night so you couldn't sleep. You have a huge exam this morning in your least favorite class. As you think about how you'll do on the test, you feel prepared.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you ready for your exam?

Undergrad scenario body: You have a big assignment due tomorrow. You've been procrastinating getting it done because it is going to be hard. When you think about whether you will be able to do the work, you think you can.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you going to complete your assignment?

Undergrad scenario body: You recently turned in a paper, and your TA asks to see you about your work. As you walk into their office, they tell you to sit down because there's lots of feedback to go through, and they look at you with a frown.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: During the meeting, you realize that your TA is taking great care to give you useful feedback and that they see this as a way to provide you with a valuable learning opportunity.
Undergrad comprehension question: Your TA probably thinks that ...

Undergrad scenario body: You have enrolled in a programming class because you think it will be a useful skill to acquire going forward. You know the demand for people with programming skills is increasing, but the course is very difficult.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: Imagining how you will do in the class, you do not see yourself failing, but instead you believe your efforts to learn some programming skills will be successful.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you think you will be effective at picking up some programming skills?

Undergrad scenario body: For a course requirement, you have to make a presentation to your classmates and your professor. As you start, you stumble on your first few words. Continuing with it, you think that your performance will be evaluated positively.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you think others will like your presentation?

Undergrad scenario body: You've recently been feeling overwhelmed with your classes, but you know that you need to get a job to be in a more stable financial situation. While searching online for jobs, you saw an application for a job that sounds really fun for you. You think that working this job on top of school will be manageable.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you think you can handle a job on top of school?

Undergrad scenario body: You have a paper due in your history class next week. You haven't started it yet because you're unsure how to answer the prompt. When you think about turning in the paper on time, you feel confident.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you worried about turning in your paper on time?

Undergrad scenario body: Your TA asks you to write a report. The finished document is quite brief but took a lot of time and effort. Based on your writing, you expect your TA's opinion of you will be negative.
Undergrad comprehension question: Your TA probably found you to be...

Undergrad scenario body: You are giving a speech at a discussion session. As you speak, you look into the class and notice that your TA's expression is almost emotionless, but you can make out a slight frown.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: After the speech, you realize that people often look emotionless and frown when they are concentrating and focusing hard. You think that your TA was most likely feeling impressed.
Undergrad comprehension question: Does your TA's neutral expression mean that they are disappointed with your speech?

Undergrad scenario body: You are taking a challenging exam. On one of the questions, you are 90% certain that you know the correct answer. When you respond, you feel confident.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you uneasy about your answer?

Undergrad scenario body: In your class today, you have an important exam. In order to pass the class, you must get at least a 75% on this exam. When you think about taking the exam, you feel prepared.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you able to pass the exam?

Undergrad scenario body: You have a midterm in your history class today. You've been studying hard for it because it counts for a big chunk of your final grade. When the TA places the test on your desk and you look at the first question, you feel prepared.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you feel ready for your midterm today?

Undergrad scenario body: You have a meeting with your professor. You're not doing well in a class required for your major. You know that your guardian will ask how you are doing in your classes later that night when you talk on the phone. When you think about what your guardian will say to you afterwards, you feel ok.
Undergrad comprehension question: Can you handle your guardian's reaction to your poor grade?

Undergrad scenario body: Some important people are visiting your class, and you are asked to present a project to them. On the day of the presentation, you arrange your slides and mentally prepare yourself. You think the visitors will evaluate your performance as organized.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will the visitors be unimpressed with your performance?

Undergrad scenario body: You are the third and final candidate to be interviewed for a scholarship. The interview panel greets you as you walk into their office. When you sit down, you feel your cheeks burning red, and you start to shake. The panel probably sees you as nervous.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: After the interview, you realize that it is normal to be nervous during panel interviews. The panelists probably saw you as reasonably calm and enthusiastic.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you think the interviewers see you as a nervous person?

Undergrad scenario body: You recently started a new job. As you've been working, it seems like everyone around you knows what they're doing, but you're still confused. You think this is because you're still learning, and you'll catch on soon.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will you get better at your job?

Undergrad scenario body: You and your hall share a printer. You have an assignment due in class today but there is a long line for the printer. When you think about printing your assignment in time, you feel confident.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will you print your assignment in time?

Undergrad scenario body: You have a big presentation in one of your classes. You know everyone's eyes are on you as you stand in front of the class. You feel nervous.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: You give yourself a moment to collect your thoughts and begin the presentation. During the presentation, you feel prepared.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you feeling prepared for your presentation?

Undergrad scenario body: You have submitted an application for a graduate school program that you have been hoping to get into. You hope to get an interview for the program after they review your application, but you know it is very competitive. With your qualifications, you expect to get a call.
Undergrad comprehension question: Your qualifications...
Undergrad correct response: Put you in a good position to get an interview.
Undergrad incorrect response: Are probably not strong enough to make you stand out.

Undergrad scenario body: Your teacher calls on you to answer a question about the book you just read. You don't know what to say. Your mind goes blank.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: You give yourself a moment to collect your thoughts and then answer the question. When you think about the answer you gave, you feel good.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you able to answer the question?

Undergrad scenario body: You got your grades back today and you got a couple lower grades than you expected. Your friends are talking about their grades, and they said they got straight As. If you tell your friends your grades, they will be understanding.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will your friends judge you for getting lower grades than them?

Undergrad scenario body: You are having a conversation with your professor in their office. You notice that you are stuttering over your words a bit more than you normally do. As the conversation continues, you decide this is okay.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you embarrassed due to stuttering over your words?

Undergrad scenario body: You don't have stable internet access at home and you found out UVA has converted to online learning. You wonder how you'll be able to keep up with your classes. As you think about the situation, you feel stressed.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you worried about keeping up with your classes?

Undergrad scenario body: You have to give a presentation to one of your classes. As you think about the presentation, you feel butterflies in your stomach. On the morning of the presentation, you feel nervous, and you think your nerves will be manageable.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you able to tolerate your anxiety?

Undergrad scenario body: Your final grades have been posted and you notice a few grades are lower than you normally get. You need a certain GPA for your major. Your major advisor may want to discuss your recent grades during your next meeting. While you're waiting for this meeting, you feel ready.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you spending your time worrying about telling your major advisor your grades?

Undergrad scenario body: You are currently going through a long week of midterms and projects. Your friend who you have not seen at all this semester has asked you to hang out the night before an exam. You know that you need to study but worry that your friend will be upset for not making time for them at all this semester.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: You realize that there is still time left in the semester to see your friend and that they will understand you need this time to study for your exam.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will your friend be mad at you for not hanging out?

Undergrad scenario body: You have a group project for one of your classes.  You've been grouped with other students who usually don't care about getting a good grade. You start to think about how the project  will go, and feel uneasy.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you worried about completing the project?

Undergrad scenario body: Your final grades have been posted and you notice a few grades are lower than you normally get. You need a certain GPA for your major. Your major advisor may want to discuss your recent grades during your next meeting. While you're waiting for this meeting, you feel ready.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you spending your time worrying about telling your major advisor your grades?

Undergrad scenario body: Your teacher calls on you to answer a question about the book you just read. You don't know what to say. Your mind goes blank.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: You give yourself a moment to collect your thoughts and then answer the question. When you think about the answer you gave, you feel good.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you able to answer the question?

Undergrad scenario body: You wake up and realize that you're late for your first online class of the day. You log into the meeting and when you do, your teacher stops talking. You apologize for being late in the chat, and spend the rest of the class feeling calm.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you anxious for the rest of class because you were late?

Undergrad scenario body: Your professer is talking about one of your role models in your field of study during a class and she asks if anyone knows about their work. In front of everyone you quickly raise your hand and the class turns to you. You realize that you feel confident.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you embarrassed that you raised your hand?

Undergrad scenario body: A friend suggests that you join an evening class on creative writing. You go online and read information about the class and see that all your work will be reveiwed by your classmates. As you navigate through the site, the thought of other people looking at your writing makes you feel enthusiastic.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you expect to feel uncomfortable if others look at your work?

Undergrad scenario body: You are told you need to give a presentation to other members of your student club. When it is time to present, you have a sip of water and wonder what the other students will think of you. As you begin to speak, you feel scared and decide to continue.
Undergrad comprehension question: When you feel anxious during a presentation...
Undergrad correct response: You have the ability to continue with your talk.
Undergrad incorrect response: You have to stop your talk and flee the situation.

Undergrad scenario body: Some important people are visiting your class, and you are asked to present a project to them. On the day of the presentation, you arrange your slides and mentally prepare yourself. You think the visitors will evaluate your performance as organized.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will the visitors be unimpressed with your performance?

Undergrad scenario body: You have a big project due at the end of the week. Your work isn't finished, but you consider taking a break to go out with friends. Taking the night off would be reasonable.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is it okay that you stopped working to go out with friends?

Undergrad scenario body: You are required to give a presentation to a group of classmates that you know well. They all are quiet during your presentation. As you think about it later in your dorm room, you think that your classmates found your presentation interesting.
Undergrad comprehension question: Your classmates were probably quiet because ...
Undergrad correct response: They were engaged by your presentation.
Undergrad incorrect response: They were bored by your presentation.

Undergrad scenario body: You are presenting your work to a group of your peers. After you present, someone asks you a very specific question that you do not know the answer to and you feel like you are unqualified. You remember that you did a lot of research, and this question may just not be relevant.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you qualified to be giving your presentation?

Undergrad scenario body: You are giving a presentation to your class about your research project. You feel that your hands are shaking a little. Wondering if it is obvious, you look around and see that a few classmates have noticed.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: You realize that your classmates are more interested in your presentation than your hands. You continue your presentation and as time passes you realize that your nerves are manageable.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you able to handle your anxiety during your presentation?

Undergrad scenario body: You've decided to go to graduate school after undergrad. You don't know anyone who is continuing their education, and all of your friends are done with school after undergraduate. When you think about your decision, you feel like it's right.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you satisfied with your decision to go to grad school?

Undergrad scenario body: You have been asked to tutor for a class. The tutor before you was extremely well liked, and you worry you won't be able to live up to students' expectations. You remember that you know the material well, and when you think about tutoring for the course, you are hopeful.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will you tutor for the course well?

Undergrad scenario body: Last month, you took on an extra job to earn more spending money. However, this has caused you to spend less time studying, and you are worried about how the job will impact your academic performance moving forward. You talk with your boss and make a plan to reduce your job hours slightly, which makes you feel relieved.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will your job have a negative impact on your schoolwork?

Undergrad scenario body: Today your top internship choice is emailing you about whether or not you were accepted. You have a few hours to wait until you'll get the email. When you think about waiting that long, you feel miserable.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you feel OK having to wait for the email?

Undergrad scenario body: Although you are normally on time for work events, today you receive an email from your boss saying that you are a few minutes late for a scheduled meeting with them, which you had completely forgotten about. As you quickly head to the office, you wonder whether they are going to judge you for being late. As you walk into the room, your anxiety is tolerable.
Undergrad comprehension question: During the meeting, your boss will probably think you are ...
Undergrad correct response: Competent, even though you made a mistake.

Undergrad scenario body: Finals just ended and you know your parents are going to want to know how you did. They have high expectations for your scores, but you did worse than you expected on a couple of your exams. Your parents will be upset.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: You think about why they want you to get high scores. You know it is because they want you to do well, so that makes you feel better.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you stay upset when thinking about telling your parents about your final scores?

Undergrad scenario body: You decide to run for president of a club you're in and you have to debate your fellow candidates in front of the school. The other candidates are on the debate team, and this is your first debate. When you think about arguing your position in front of your other club members, you feel confident.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you worried about the debate?

Undergrad scenario body: You have started a new part time job and have had a difficult time managing it with school. Your boss asked if you would be willing to help her out with a project outside of your usual responsibilities, and you are worried if she will be dissappointed if you say no. After thinking on it, you tell her that you can't help out with this project because of school, but are willing to help out on the next one.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is it okay to turn down additional responsibility?

Undergrad scenario body: You are giving a presentation in class and accidentally skip a section so have to start again. You are embarrassed by the mistake and worry what the audience thought of you. The audience probably thinks your delivery after starting over was awkward.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: Later that day, you realize that it is common to make mistakes when presenting. Your classmates probably thought your delivery after starting over was admirable.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you think that your classmates thought of you as awkward for making a mistake?

Undergrad scenario body: You have had a lot of assignments and exams pile up these past couple of weeks. As a result, you have not been able to spend time with friends or family as much as you would want and have often times felt lonely. Once your workload lessens, you plan on reconnecting with people who mean a lot to you.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will you be able to hang out with friends and family when you have more time?

Undergrad scenario body: You are taking a class and recently turned in a paper. You decide to re-read your submitted paper, and realize that you accidentally deleted a section you meant to include in the paper. Given that you accidentally made this deletion, you think that you are likely to pass.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you going to get a passing grade on the paper?

Undergrad scenario body: You are taking a test in a class. You only have ten minutes left and you still have three long problems to get through. When you think about your ability to complete the test, you feel doubtful.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will you be able to finish your test in time?

Undergrad scenario body: You are giving a speech at a discussion session. As you speak, you look into the class and notice that your TA's expression is almost emotionless, but you can make out a slight frown.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: After the speech, you realize that people often look emotionless and frown when they are concentrating and focusing hard. You think that your TA was most likely feeling impressed.
Undergrad comprehension question: Does your TA's neutral expression mean that they are disappointed with your speech?

Undergrad scenario body: You recently saw on the news that the economy has been particularly bad. Even though you have an above-average resume, you wonder whether you'll be able to find a job after graduating. You think you will likely be fine.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is it likely that you will find a job?

Undergrad scenario body: This year, you've decied to take a 4000 level class. This is your first 4000 class and you've heard from your classmates that it's a lot of work. When you think about the class, you think that you will be able to do well.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will you succeed in your first 4000 level class?

Undergrad scenario body: Your family wants you to pursue a specific major. You would rather choose a different major but your family has made it clear they are not on board with the major choice. When you think about talking with your family about your choice, you feel prepared.
Undergrad comprehension question: Can you handle talking to your family about your major choice?

Undergrad scenario body: Your professor calls on you to answer a question about the book you just read. You don't know what to say, and your mind goes blank. After a few moments, you prepare your answer and feel ok.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you able to answer the question?

Undergrad scenario body: You have an essay to write, and you try very hard to make sure it is comprehensive. The final copy is quite long. When your professor reads it, you think they will judge your efforts as suitable.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will your professor judge your efforts as reasonable?

Undergrad scenario body: You just turned in an important paper. As you're checking your submission, you see a typo in the first sentence. You believe that you should be concerned.
Undergrad comprehension question: Should you be worried about the typo?

Undergrad scenario body: Your professor is leading a discussion on creating areas of focus for the final project. You are suddenly asked to contribute your ideas to the discussion. You quickly come up with some ideas on the spot and think that your professor and the class will find your ideas to be exciting.
Undergrad comprehension question: The people in your class will probably ...

Undergrad scenario body: You finish a task due next week for your evening class and ask the tutor for his opinion. They say the work is good, apart from an incomplete section. You feel that they think you are careless.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: In the end, you realize that the tutor brought the error to your attention so you could improve it before turning it in. You fix the incomplete section and believe that the tutor thinks you are learning.
Undergrad comprehension question: Was your tutor pleased with your progress?

Undergrad scenario body: You have a lot of work to do for one of your major classes. One of your most important homework assignments for the class is to do research about a topic and make a well-prepared, lengthy presentation to your classmates. As you imagine yourself giving your presentation, you feel excited.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you looking forward to giving your presentation in your class?

Undergrad scenario body: In the hallway outside of the classroom, your professor asks you a question, and you realize you respond incorrectly. Your professor corrects you. You think the experience of being corrected by your professor is awful.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: In the end, you remind yourself that occasionally making mistakes does not make you a bad student. Your professor had corrected you kindly, as was their job, and you feel that the whole interaction went well.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is it okay to occasionally answer a question from your professor incorrectly?

Undergrad scenario body: This semester, a few new members joined your club. Your club's president asks you to stay after a meeting to give them a brief presentation about the club's goals and values but tells you that this is completely optional. You feel a little nervous about giving the presentation, and you decide to stay.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you stay to give the optional presentation?

Undergrad scenario body: You have received a poor score on an exam and know that your guardian will ask how you did on the exam. They frown when you tell them your exam grade. You tell them that you studied extremely hard despite the grade, and they are proud of the effort you put into the exam.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is your guardian disappointed that you got a bad grade?

Undergrad scenario body: You consider taking an evening class in which part of your grade is based on your participation, even though you do not like speaking up in front of others. Because the topic is really interesting, you decide to sign up for the class. After signing up, you realize that this decision was great.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you happy that you signed up for the interesting evening class?

Undergrad scenario body: A recruiter for your dream internship is watching your class presentations. During the presentation, you stumble over your words a lot. You feel defeated.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: While you feel upset, you realize that it's natural to make mistakes. You think that the recruiter won't remember.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is it okay that you made a mistake in front of the recruiter?

Undergrad scenario body: This morning you hear the news that a lot of classes are currently limiting their number of students. You are in your last year of undergrad and you think about what will happen if you cannot enroll for the classes you need to finish your major this semester. You decide that finding classes next semester will be possible.
Undergrad comprehension question: If you are unable to enroll in a class you need for this semester, you will probably ...
Undergrad correct response: Wait until next semester to enroll.
Undergrad incorrect response: Be unable to graduate on time.

Undergrad scenario body: You are in class, and the professor asks all students to stand up and introduce themselves. During your brief presentation, you momentarily forget what you planned to say, and the class stares at you looking annoyed.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: In the end, you realize that everyone has these moments, and you quickly recover. As you finish introducing yourself, you see that the others are listening with keen interest.
Undergrad comprehension question: Did the other people think you were boring?

Undergrad scenario body: You are giving a presentation in class. During one of your slides, you lose your train of thought and have to take a moment to look over your notes. As you start again, you think that losing one's train of thought while presenting is normal.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is it normal to lose your train of thought while presenting?

Undergrad scenario body: You had a very busy day, and were not able to get all of your tasks done. While at dinner with a friend, they brag about how they finished everything on their to-do list. Thinking about whether you can complete your work tomorrow, you feel confident.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will you get everything done?

Undergrad scenario body: Your Professor calls on you to answer a question. As you start to talk you hear a loud burst of laughter behind you. Your classmates are laughing at a joke.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are your classmates laughing at you?

Undergrad scenario body: Today, you will receive an important email. It will tell you whether you got accepted to or rejected from the summer intership you really want to do. As you think about waiting for the email, you feel excited.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you miserable waiting for the important email?

Undergrad scenario body: You just started at a new job and you work with lots of new people. You hear a few of them laughing together and talking about going out to dinner, but they don't invite you along. As you think about your future with this group, you feel okay.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is it too late to befriend your new coworkers?

Undergrad scenario body: You have recieved a poor score on an exam and tell your parents over the phone. They don't say anything when you tell them your grade. They are probably feeling disappointed.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are your parents disappointed that you got a bad grade?

Undergrad scenario body: A new professor is hired for the class you are looking forward to the most, and you hear that he is a tough grader. You meet him for the first time and talk about the class. Getting up to leave at the end of your meeting, you think that he found you to be intelligent.
Undergrad comprehension question: Does your new professor have a good opinion of you?

Undergrad scenario body: You just finished a hard exam, and you are not sure whether you did well or not. You feel nervous as you think about what grade you will get. You think being nervous is manageable.
Undergrad comprehension question: Can you tolerate your nervousness as you wait to find out your grade?

Undergrad scenario body: While working in a group for a project you tell your ideas to your group. The other members of the group don't react. They must be thinking about the project.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do the members of the group think your ideas are bad?

Undergrad scenario body: You recently decided to join a new club on top of being on the executive team in another club. At a meeting for the new club, you brought the wrong notebook and have notes for the other club, which aren't useful here. You think managing being in both clubs will be difficult.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will it be hard to be in both clubs?

Undergrad scenario body: You studied hard for an important test. You see that you got a lower score than you expected. You think about whether you will be able to pull your grade up with the next test, and realize this was the last test.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: Right now you aren't happy, but you think about your overall course grade. You realize that you will pass.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will you fail the class?

Undergrad scenario body: In class, you have been assigned a group project worth a large part of your grade. The due date is soon, and your groupmates still haven't completed their portion of the work. You reach out to them, and they begin working in a timely manner.
Undergrad comprehension question: Your groupmates are likely...
Undergrad correct response: Grateful that you politely communicated your concerns over their contribution.
Undergrad incorrect response: Annoyed that you're telling them what to do.

Undergrad scenario body: You have almost completed an important course. Part of your grade will be determined by a presentation that is to be graded by your classmates. You know most of them and feel that their opinions of you will make them grade your work more favorably.
Undergrad comprehension question: Your classmates will probably ...

Undergrad scenario body: You are preparing to apply to graduate school. You have a meeting with a potential advisor tomorrow, and you know that a good resume will be an important factor.  As you look over your resume, you think that the potential advisor will be favorable.
Undergrad comprehension question: Your potential advisor will probably think
Undergrad correct response: That your previous experience is a good fit for the job.
Undergrad incorrect response: That your listed skills are unimpressive.

Undergrad scenario body: You are sitting in your online classroom and space out for a minute. Your professor calls on you to answer a question and you didn't hear it. You ask her to repeat her question and this makes you feel ok.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you comfortable asking your professor  to repeat her question?

Undergrad scenario body: You want to talk to someone about your plans for the future, but don't want to talk to your guardian about it. You think about other adults in your life, and decide to talk to your mentor. When you think about their reaction, you think they will probably be encouraging.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you feel worried about talking to your mentor about your plans for the future?

Undergrad scenario body: You are in an important meeting and you need to pay attention. Halfway through the meeting you realize you have been zoning out and don't know what is going on. You think something must be wrong with you.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: You take a few sips of water and start to feel more awake, remembering that you didn't get as much sleep as usual last night. You decide you are just tired.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is your zoning out a sign of something wrong with your brain?

Undergrad scenario body: Your new teaching assistant calls a meeting to meet everyone and asks each of you in turn to present yourself and your prior experience. When it is your turn to speak, you pause frequently. As you reflect on the meeting later in the day, you feel that the teaching assistant thought you sounded capable.
Undergrad comprehension question: Your teaching assistant probably found you to be ...

Undergrad scenario body: You are giving a presentation in class and accidentally skip a section so have to start again. You are embarrassed by the mistake and worry what the audience thought of you. The audience probably thinks your delivery after starting over was awkward.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: Later that day, you realize that it is common to make mistakes when presenting. Your classmates probably thought your delivery after starting over was admirable.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you think that your classmates thought of you as awkward for making a mistake?

Undergrad scenario body: You are in class on the first day of school and reach to put your phone away as the professor starts talking. Your professor mistakes this for you texting during class and calls you out in front of the whole class. You go up to apologize after class and you can't read your professor's expression.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: After explaining what happened to your professor, he says he understands. You feel like you will still be able make a good impression.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will your professor view you negatively because of this?

Undergrad scenario body: You have a midterm in your history class today. You've been studying hard for it because it counts for a big chunk of your final grade. When the TA places the test on your desk and you look at the first question, you feel prepared.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you feel ready for your midterm today?

Undergrad scenario body: Work has been piling up in school recently, and you were going to take the weekend to catch up. However, your friend reminded you of an event this weekend that you committed to, and it would be rude to cancel last minute. You think that completing your work will be doable.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will you be able to do work this weekend?

Undergrad comprehension question: During class, your peers will probably think

Undergrad comprehension question: Do you feel capable of completing the work for this class?

Undergrad comprehension question: Did you need to spend money on a new outfit?

When you look into flights, you realize that the cost of a trip may be totally out of your budget. You remind yourself you can go for a short trip somewhere nearby and still have fun.

Undergrad scenario body: As you are walking down a crowded street, you see your classmate on the other side. You call out, but they do not answer you. Standing there in the street, you think that this must be because they were distracted.
Undergrad comprehension question: Did your classmate purposely ignore your call to them in the street?

Undergrad scenario body: You just moved to a new city and have met a lot of really interesting people. However, you feel like you still don't have a very close group of friends to rely on and miss your close friends from your old city. You decide to spend more time with the people you have met so you can become closer friends.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you optimistic when you think about your new friends?

Undergrad scenario body: It's finally Friday, and you overhear some classmates talking about their plans for the weekend. You didn't have anything big planned for the weekend, and you feel like you aren't social enough to be in college. When you imagine relaxing after a hard week of work, you feel content.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you have to be more social?

Undergrad scenario body: You are in class, and the professor asks all students to stand up and introduce themselves. During your brief presentation, you momentarily forget what you planned to say, and the class stares at you looking annoyed.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: In the end, you realize that everyone has these moments, and you quickly recover. As you finish introducing yourself, you see that the others are listening with keen interest.
Undergrad comprehension question: Did the other people think you were boring?

Undergrad scenario body: You have been very busy and are having trouble keeping in contact with one of your best friends. You are usually good about calling them, but your schedule has been very hectic. As you think about their reaction to this lull in contact, you think that your friend will be upset.
Undergrad comprehension question: Since you have been out of touch, your friend is probably thinking
Undergrad correct response: "I guess this friendship isn't worth very much."
Undergrad incorrect response: "I'm sure things are just really busy right now."

Undergrad scenario body: You just moved to a new apartment and you are living alone for the first time. Your days are very quiet and you often find yourself staying in on the weekends with much fewer plans than you had when you were living with people. This feels like a relaxing change of pace.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you bored living alone?

Undergrad scenario body: You are told you need to give a presentation to other members of your student club. When it is time to present, you have a sip of water and wonder what the other students will think of you. As you begin to speak, you feel scared and decide to continue.
Undergrad comprehension question: When you feel anxious during a presentation...
Undergrad correct response: You have the ability to continue with your talk.
Undergrad incorrect response: You have to stop your talk and flee the situation.

Undergrad scenario body: You go to see a movie on opening night. When you get to the theater, you notice that you are the only person sitting alone. You feel excited for the movie to start.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you okay with sitting alone?

Undergrad scenario body: When you think about the next 50 years, you wonder which friends will still be a part of your life. You hope to keep in touch with your closest friends but know that it will be difficult.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: When you imagine yourself in 50 years, you do not think you will have drifted from your good friends, and you see you and your close friends together.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you expect to be close to your current friends in the future?

Undergrad scenario body: You are giving a presentation in class. During one of your slides, you lose your train of thought and have to take a moment to look over your notes. As you start again, you think that losing one's train of thought while presenting is normal.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is it normal to lose your train of thought while presenting?

Undergrad scenario body: Your friend is having a huge birthday party with all of your friends attending. You know you can't afford a gift for your friend. You show up with a homemade card, your friend is thankful.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is your friend sad you did not bring a present?

Undergrad scenario body: Your best friend is organizing a birthday party for you at their home. They invited a lot of people who will all be there for you. When you think about all of the people coming, you feel excited.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you excited about having people there to celebrate your birthday?

Undergrad scenario body: You are put in charge of your club's fundraising efforts and quickly realize that the members are not very interested in the fundraising. At a meeting, you give a presentation about the importance of helping with the fundraising, during which the members remain quiet. As you sit down after your presentation, you think the members seemed bored.
Undergrad comprehension question: The club members are probably thinking ...
Undergrad correct response: That they don't want to help you with the fundraiser.
Undergrad incorrect response: That they want to help you with the fundraiser.

Undergrad scenario body: In class, you have been assigned a group project worth a large part of your grade. The due date is soon, and your groupmates still haven't completed their portion of the work. You reach out to them, and they begin working in a timely manner.
Undergrad comprehension question: Your groupmates are likely...
Undergrad correct response: Grateful that you politely communicated your concerns over their contribution.
Undergrad incorrect response: Annoyed that you're telling them what to do.

Undergrad scenario body: As you scroll through your social media, you see that your friends hung out without you last night. You didn't get an invite to hang out with them. You realize that sometimes your friends hang out without you and you feel excluded.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: You don't like being left out. But, you decide that you can tolerate this feeling.
Undergrad comprehension question: Can you handle that sometimes your friends hang out without you?

Undergrad scenario body: You notice there are a lot of attractive people posting on TikTok. You are a bit uncomfortable posting a video, but post anyway. You feel confident.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you hold back from posting a video because you do not feel as attractive as the people you see in other videos?

Undergrad scenario body: It's your first day of a new semester with all new classes, and it's time for you to eat lunch. A group of students goes quiet after you ask them if you can sit at their table. They are probably nice.
Undergrad comprehension question: Were the quiet students nice?

Undergrad scenario body: It is a long weekend, and you are invited to a friend's wedding. When you arrive to the venue you notice that many people have brought a plus one, and you came alone. You realize that you can still enjoy yourself at the wedding and get to know other guests even while being alone.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you enjoy yourself at the wedding?

Undergrad scenario body: At a dinner party, you are introduced to someone new and chat with them for quite a while. When you call them the next week to suggest meeting again, they pause for a moment. They probably think that getting together would be great.
Undergrad comprehension question: Does your new acquaintance want to meet with you again?

Undergrad scenario body: Your housing is smaller and not as stylish or well organized as those of others that you know. Your friend has never been to your place, but wants to come over and spend the night. You think their reaction to your home will be respectful.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will your friends judge your home?

Undergrad scenario body: You organize a day outside with your friends. The outing does not go very well, and you feel discouraged. As you head home, you think that others thought the event did not go well because of the rain.
Undergrad comprehension question: Your friends did not enjoy the outing because ...

Undergrad scenario body: This semester, a few new members joined your club. Your club's president asks you to stay after a meeting to give them a brief presentation about the club's goals and values but tells you that this is completely optional. You feel a little nervous about giving the presentation, and you decide to stay.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you stay to give the optional presentation?

Undergrad scenario body: It's Friday afternoon and you have no plans for the rest of the night. You think about what your friends are doing and if they are currently enjoying themselves. You wonder if they forgot about you.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: You realize it has been a busy week and they are probably resting. You and your friends often take breaks after a long week in order to rest.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are your friends ignoring you?

Undergrad scenario body: You are giving a presentation in class and accidentally skip a section so have to start again. You are embarrassed by the mistake and worry what the audience thought of you. The audience probably thinks your delivery after starting over was awkward.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: Later that day, you realize that it is common to make mistakes when presenting. Your classmates probably thought your delivery after starting over was admirable.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you think that your classmates thought of you as awkward for making a mistake?

Undergrad scenario body: You are at a birthday party for a friend and talking with someone you just met. You cannot tell whether the person thinks you are interesting or boring, and you decide that not knowing is okay.
Undergrad comprehension question: Overall, the conversation was probably a ...
Undergrad correct response: Low-pressure and pleasant chance to meet a new person.
Undergrad incorrect response: Stressful situation, because the person might have found you uninteresting.

Undergrad scenario body: You are at out of town visiting friends and decide to go out dancing. Walking into a nightclub for the first time, you feel that the things around you are very unreal. This is interesting.
Undergrad comprehension question: The lights and sounds of the club make you feel ...

Undergrad scenario body: You are having a conversation with your best friend of many years. During the conversation, they criticize you, saying that they don't think your major is worth studying. You wonder whether you should remain friends with them.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: After thinking about it, you realize that people don't have to agree with everything you do. You love your friend and will continue to be friends with them.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you going to let a disagreement ruin your friendship?

Undergrad scenario body: Your professer is talking about one of your role models in your field of study during a class and she asks if anyone knows about their work. In front of everyone you quickly raise your hand and the class turns to you. You realize that you feel confident.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you embarrassed that you raised your hand?

Undergrad scenario body: You meet a cute classmate in one of your classes. After class, they request to follow you on social media. Before you let them follow you, you look over your own feed and feel  confident.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you worried about what the other person thinks of your feed?

Undergrad scenario body: You invite your roommate to visit you and your family.  While you're eating dinner together, your family switches back and forth between English and another language. You never told your roommate that your family members are bilingual. You think about your roommate's reaction, and think that she'll think it is interesting.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is your roommate judgmental of your bilingual family?

Undergrad scenario body: An old acquaintance just moved to Charlottesville and asks you out to coffee. You are nervous about seeing them again after many years and could easily tell them that you are too busy. You are in your dorm when they call about meeting, and you decline the call.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you take your acquaintance’s call to meet for coffee?

Undergrad scenario body: For your vacation this year you are visiting a foreign country for the first time. When you are there, everything around you feels different and strange. You think this is awesome.
Undergrad comprehension question: In this country, you think you will find...
Undergrad correct response: Opportunities to learn and experience the culture.

Undergrad scenario body: A group of your friends are whispering and looking in your direction. Some of them are laughing really hard. You don't have time to go say hi, and you think they were probably laughing at a joke.
Undergrad comprehension question: Were they laughing at you?

Undergrad scenario body: Over the weekend you decide to post a video and a few pictures on your social media. When you get to a club meeting on Monday, you overhear other members gossiping about your pictures and videos. They probably think that you are cool.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you worried about what your classmates are saying?

Undergrad scenario body: You are giving a presentation during your evening class. As you talk, you hear a few of your classmates laughing in the corner. You look over at them and realize they were probably laughing at you.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: After a moment, you remember situations where you and your classmates have laughed about things unrelated to who is speaking. You move on from their laughter and complete your presentation successfully.
Undergrad comprehension question: Were your classmates laughing at you?

Undergrad scenario body: You arrive to class with your mask on. You notice that one of your classmates is not wearing a mask. You think that they must have forgotten it.
Undergrad comprehension question: Did your classmate forget their mask?

Undergrad scenario body: A group of your friends are going out to dinner and they invited you. You know that you all have been going out to dinner a lot recently, and you don't know if you can keep up with this lifestyle. You tell your friends that you'll catch up with them next time, and you're happy staying at home for the night.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you comfortable passing up on this dinner?

Undergrad scenario body: You and your friends are comparing scores on a social media app. You have a low score and your friends start teasing you. You begin to feel insecure.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: You don't use the app often and that's why your score is lower than theirs. You know your friends know this and they weren't trying to be mean.
Undergrad comprehension question: Were your friends trying to make you feel insecure about having a low score on the social media app?

Undergrad scenario body: You have been invited to a friend's house for a holiday and you want to bring a gift, but can't afford to buy one. You decide to write a poem and recite it for their family. As you picture yourself reading it, you feel excited.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you worried about reciting a poem for your friend's family?

Undergrad scenario body: You and some friends are watching a movie at your home. At the end of the movie, your friend asks what that funny smell is. You know that's your dinner, and when you think about how to answer you feel hungry.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you anxious about telling your friend that the smell is your dinner?

Undergrad scenario body: The person you have a crush on starts sending you funny pictures on social media. Today you notice they opened your last picture and didn't respond. They must have forgotten.
Undergrad comprehension question: Did your crush forget to respond to you?

Undergrad scenario body: You're meeting a friend at a local park. After 20 minutes, your friend still hasn't arrived. You think they probably are coming.
Undergrad comprehension question: Did your friend stand you up?

Undergrad scenario body: You are at a party with friends and one of your friends is intoxicated. When you suggest that it's time to go home, they say no and walk away from you. You think that they are having fun.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is your friend upset with you?

Undergrad scenario body: You invite your classmate over after class and your roommate is home. Your roommate is pretty opinionated and may say something embarrassing to your classmate. As you and your classmate walk in, you are calm.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you anxious as you and your classmate walk in?

Undergrad scenario body: You meet a new classmate in your online class and they ask for your name on social media. When you tell them that you don't have that app, they grow quiet. They coud be brainstorming other ways to contact you, which makes you feel happy.
Undergrad comprehension question: Did your classmate get quiet because they were judging you?

Undergrad scenario body: During your class over Zoom, you're placed into breakout rooms. When you start the discussion, your camera is on but everyone else leaves their cameras off and their mics muted. You think that the others will think that you are ready.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are the others in the class judging you?

Undergrad scenario body: You are taking a dancing class and, during a break, find yourself talking to a newcomer about your major. As you chat, you notice that you are sweating. They probably assume this is a result of dancing.
Undergrad comprehension question: Did the person you met at dance class think you were anxious?

Undergrad scenario body: You are currently going through a long week of midterms and projects. Your friend who you have not seen at all this semester has asked you to hang out the night before an exam. You know that you need to study but worry that your friend will be upset for not making time for them at all this semester.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: You realize that there is still time left in the semester to see your friend and that they will understand you need this time to study for your exam.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will your friend be mad at you for not hanging out?

Undergrad scenario body: You are outside jogging and see a group of people ahead of you. As you pass them, you stumble slightly and feel a little foolish. You hear them laugh as you pass by, and assume that they are laughing at jokes.
Undergrad comprehension question: The group of runners was probably laughing because ...

Undergrad scenario body: You arrange to meet up with a friend you have not seen for many years. You drive to the train station to pick them up. When you arrive, you know they will find spending time with you fun.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you think your friend will have a good time with you?

Undergrad scenario body: You are having a conversation with your professor in their office. You notice that you are stuttering over your words a bit more than you normally do. As the conversation continues, you decide this is okay.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you embarrassed due to stuttering over your words?

Undergrad scenario body: Your friend invites you to a party and after you leave, they casually mention that one of the people there had been exposed and was waiting on a test result. You can’t believe your friend wouldn’t tell you this before they took you to the party. You decide to talk to your friend about it and you both decide to isolate until the test comes back and communicate better about risky situations.
Undergrad comprehension question: Did your friend put you in a dangerous situation on purpose?

Undergrad scenario body: You're called up to the front of the class to give your presentation. You stumble as you get up from your chair. When you think about everyone seeing you stumble, you feel embarrassed.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you okay with people seeing you stumble?

Undergrad scenario body: You are new to a class, and your professor asks you to do a task. You finish it before the deadline and your professor immediately points out that you made a mistake. Your professor probably thinks you are a failure.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: In the end, you realize that your professor is prompt in giving you feedback because they are investing in you, and interested in seeing you progress.
Undergrad comprehension question: Does your professor believe that your work holds promise?

Undergrad scenario body: You check your score on a social media app, and your score is getting really high. If your score gets much higher, people will think that you use the app a lot. This makes you feel unbothered.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you care that people know you use this app a lot?

Undergrad scenario body: You got your grades back today and you got a couple lower grades than you expected. Your friends are talking about their grades, and they said they got straight As. If you tell your friends your grades, they will be understanding.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will your friends judge you for getting lower grades than them?

Undergrad scenario body: You arrive at a party in a new outfit. Everyone turns to look at you as you walk in. You decide that they must think that you look stylish.
Undergrad comprehension question: Did everyone at the party like your new outfit?

Undergrad scenario body: Tomorrow is a close friend's birthday. You forgot to get them a gift and don't have much time to shop for something good. You pick up something mediocre from a nearby store and you think they will be upset.
Undergrad comprehension question: You imagine that when you show up at your friend's birthday, they will think
Undergrad correct response: "This is almost worse than no gift at all."
Undergrad incorrect response: "Aw. It was really nice of my friend to bring me a gift."

Undergrad scenario body: While at the hairdresser's, you opt for a completely different haircut. When you see your friend afterwards, they gasp. Their gasp probably means that they think the new style makes you look great.
Undergrad comprehension question: Does your friend like your new haircut?

Undergrad scenario body: One afternoon you are hanging out with a friend, and you notice that the conversation has died down. Neither of you says anything for a while, and your friend can sense that you feel uncomfortable.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: That evening, you realize that it is completely normal to have silent breaks during conversations. You believe that your friend thinks it was better to stay silent than to make up things to say, and was probably glad.
Undergrad comprehension question: Does your friend think you are okay with silence?

Undergrad scenario body: An old acquaintance just moved to Charlottesville and asks you out to coffee. You are nervous about seeing them again after many years and could easily tell them that you are too busy. You are in your dorm when they call about meeting, and you decide to answer.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you take your acquaintance’s call to meet for coffee?

Undergrad scenario body: You have been taking a dance class at the local community center, and the time has come for your end of class showcase. You want to invite your friends and family to the showcase, but you worry you'll embarrass yourself if you are not very good.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: While deciding whether or not to invite people to your show, you think you won't have a bad performance, and you see yourself doing fine.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you think you'll do poorly at your dance showcase?

Undergrad scenario body: You see a new post on you social media app and realize some of your friends are hanging out right now. You don't remember being invited to hangout with them. You think about missing out on the fun, and think that you weren't invited because you are busy.
Undergrad comprehension question: Did they exclude you because they don't like you?

Undergrad scenario body: After moving in with your friend, you realize they often ignore chores. One Friday, you come home to see a pile of dirty dishes in the sink and your friend has left for the weekend already. You decide to call them and explain why you are upset.
Undergrad comprehension question: Should you ignore this problem?

Undergrad scenario body: You have a lot of work to do for one of your major classes. One of your most important homework assignments for the class is to do research about a topic and make a well-prepared, lengthy presentation to your classmates. As you imagine yourself giving your presentation, you feel excited.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you looking forward to giving your presentation in your class?

Undergrad scenario body: You are at home and it's the first weekend you've had free time in a while. After texting a friend to make plans several hours ago, you notice that they still haven't responded. You see that they have been active on their social media accounts, and wonder if they're ignoring you or don't want to spend time with you.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: After thinking about it, you recognize that your friend is a busy person, and that people miss notifications all the time. You bump your message, and find that your friend had forgotten to respond, but is very eager to make plans with you.
Undergrad comprehension question: Does not receiving a prompt response mean that your friend doesn't like you?

Undergrad scenario body: On your way out from class, you stop in the restroom. As you leave the bathroom stall, your classmate passes you and gives you a funny look. Your classmate is probably distracted.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is your classmate negatively judging you as you leave the restroom?

Undergrad scenario body: You are in the car with your close friend's mother. As you discuss which station to listen to, you realize that you and their mother have musical tastes that are wildly clashing.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: After having a discussion about the pros and cons of different music styles, you realize that she probably does not mind that your tastes clash and simply sees you as knowledgeable.
Undergrad comprehension question: Does your close friend's mother think you know nothing about music?

Undergrad scenario body: After being inactive for a few years, you recently joined a recreational soccer league. There is a tournament at the end of the season. You believe that you will contribute to your team's failure.
Undergrad comprehension question: At the end of the game, your teammates are likely to be thinking

Undergrad scenario body: You are walking to an important meeting with your professor. Suddenly your mind goes blank as you reach for the doorknob to her office. You think about your mind going blank and think that this is normal.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is it scary that your mind went blank right before your meeting?

Hoos TC Title: Sharing Social Media with New Friend
Undergrad scenario body: You meet a new classmate in your online class and they ask you for your name on social media. When you tell them you don't have that app, they grow quiet. They must think that you are different, and that's alright.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is it okay that your classmate thinks you're different?

Undergrad scenario body: When you think about the next 50 years, you wonder which friends will still be a part of your life. You hope to keep in touch with your closest friends but know that it will be difficult. When you imagine yourself in 50 years, you see you and your close friends having stayed close.
Undergrad comprehension question: You think that staying close to your current friends
Undergrad correct response: Will be something that brings you happiness for years to come.
Undergrad incorrect response: Is almost impossible in the long run.

Undergrad scenario body: Your favorite part of the day is winding down to eat dinner. When you start making dinner, you realize that you've been eating alone a lot recently, and you feel sad. You decide you are okay with this fact, as it gives you time to reflect on your day.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you okay with eating dinner alone?

Undergrad scenario body: You are eating in the dining hall and see your friend getting food. They walk past you and sit with someone else. They must have been distracted.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is your friend upset with you?

Undergrad scenario body: Your family shares one older car that has a big dent in the front. Today your parent is helping move you in to your dorm. When you think about your roomates seeing your parent's car, you feel nervous.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: As you're getting out of the car in front of the dorm, you see a group of people. They smile and wave at you, and you feel relieved.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do they judge your parent's car?

Undergrad scenario body: Your professor has asked you to give a presentation about your latest project to the rest of the class. This is a great opportunity to highlight the good work you have been doing, but you feel nervous talking in front of such a big group of your peers. As you think about how your peers will react to your presentation, you believe they will think you looked confident.
Undergrad comprehension question: During class, your peers will probably think
Undergrad correct response: You do a good job explaining your recent work.
Undergrad incorrect response: You look uncomfortable giving your presentation.

Undergrad scenario body: You are on your way home after an IM-Rec soccer game that you played in. As your mind wanders, you think about the one goal that you missed. Overall, you consider your performance a success.
Undergrad comprehension question: Reflecting on the soccer game, do you think you played well?

Undergrad scenario body: You and your best friend have known each other since middle school. You are starting to have some different interests, and you worry that you won't understand each other as you get older. When you think about major life events, such as your wedding or having kids, you expect your best friend to be unsupportive.
Undergrad comprehension question: Years from now, you expect you and your friend
Undergrad correct response: Will have grown apart and not really understand one another.
Undergrad incorrect response: Will have stayed close even though you have developed some different interests.

Undergrad scenario body: You are in class on the first day of school and reach to put your phone away as the professor starts talking. Your professor mistakes this for you texting during class and calls you out in front of the whole class. You go up to apologize after class and you can't read your professor's expression.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: After explaining what happened to your professor, he says he understands. You feel like you will still be able make a good impression.
Undergrad comprehension question: Will your professor view you negatively because of this?

Undergrad scenario body: You are trying to meet new people and you decide to join a book club. The book for the week is really interesting, but when you show up at the meeting people are asking questions about the book that seem more in depth than you can answer. You remember that you read the book too and have valuable thoughts just like everyone else.
Undergrad comprehension question: Can you be involved in the book club discussion?

Undergrad scenario body: You posted a new picture a few hours ago. You check to see who's liked it and notice that your best friend hasn't liked it yet. You think about how your best friend doesn't check their feed often and you feel better.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is it okay that your best friend hasn't liked your post yet?

Undergrad scenario body: One evening, your friends from class decide to go out to eat and ask you to join them. You accept, but will have to wear the outfit you wore to class. When you arrive, the other diners turn to look at you. You think this is because they think you look stylish.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you think that other diners think you are dressed inappropriately?

Undergrad scenario body: You have been taking a dance class at the local community center, and the time has come for your end of class showcase. You want to invite your friends and family to the showcase, but you worry you'll embarrass yourself if you are not very good. While deciding whether or not to invite people to your show, you see yourself doing well.
Undergrad comprehension question: Based on how you see yourself performing, inviting your friends and family is probably

Undergrad scenario body: You buy a new suit, which is very different from your usual style of clothes. You try it on to show your friend. When they see you, they pause. They then comment that it makes you look nice.
Undergrad comprehension question: Did your friend think your new suit looked foolish?

Undergrad scenario body: You are in a theater organization on Grounds, which is planning a new production. At the first meeting, the producer asks you for ideas about which play to perform. Everybody pauses after you say your ideas, and you think the others find your thoughts boring.
Undergrad comprehension question: Everyone probably believes ...

Undergrad scenario body: Your friend is having a get together tonight. You weren't invited, but most of your other friends were. When you think about it, you realize it's because you are busy.
Undergrad comprehension question: Were you not invited because you were busy?

Undergrad scenario body: As you scroll through your feed on social media, you see a photo of your friends that shows them having a great time together over video chat. You weren't invited to hang out with them online. You think about it, and decide that you can cope.
Undergrad comprehension question: Can you handle that sometimes your friends hang out without you?

Undergrad scenario body: You are in a store and accidentally knock over a few picture frames in one of the aisles. You feel your cheeks become hot as people in the store turn and look at you. When the shop owner comes over to rearrange the picture frames, they are frowning.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: After a few moments, the other shoppers turn away; they were just startled by the loud noise. The store owner kindly accepts your apology and tells you not to worry about the mess, so you then feel calm.
Undergrad comprehension question: The other shoppers probably think you are ...

Undergrad scenario body: Your roommate hasn't been home much recently. However, when they do come home, they leave their garbage on the table, crumbs on the counter, and never lock the door on their way out. You think it's time to bring it up to them.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: When you bring it up, they explain that they've been extremely overwhelmed recently and it's been hard for them to keep up with things. You come to an agreement on how to keep the space clean.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you mad at your roommate?

Undergrad scenario body: You get invited to a pool party and you decide to wear a suit you got last year. You arrive and everyone else seems to have a swimsuit that is brand new. As you walk around, the host comes up to you and says you look nice.
Undergrad comprehension question: Does the host of the party think you look bad?

Undergrad scenario body: You are at a birthday party with some friends. Later in the evening, your friend asks you to give a toast even though you don't have anything prepared. Afterwards, you feel that the others found your toast entertaining.
Undergrad comprehension question: Did your friends enjoy your toast?

Undergrad scenario body: You're at the thrift store with your friends and you're looking through the clothes. You notice two people from your evening class in the other part of the store laughing. They are probably laughing at a joke.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are your peers laughing at you?

Undergrad scenario body: A good friend of yours told you they'd meet you at your favorite restaurant for dinner one night. You text them that morning to ask what time to meet, but they haven't answered yet and its now early afternoon, and you're quite upset. You think they probably are just busy at work.

Undergrad scenario body: You are at a birthday party for a friend of a friend. You notice that you are flushed and a little sweaty. Standing there, you think that these sensations are normal.
Undergrad comprehension question: People will probably ...
Undergrad correct response: Not see you sweat since the room is dimly lit.

Undergrad scenario body: You see an old friend you haven't spoken to in years. Your heart skips a beat. You feel excited.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is it OK that your heart felt like it skipped a beat?

Undergrad scenario body: You are working on a project with a classmate at a small table in a coffee shop, and when your coffee arrives, you accidentally spill it all over their papers, which makes them jump in distress.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: After you clean up the mess as best as you can, you realize that their response was a normal, knee-jerk reaction. They say that they are not upset with you, and you believe that they are sincere.
Undergrad comprehension question: Your classmate is probably ...

Undergrad scenario body: There's a personal question that you've been wanting to ask your advisor for a while, but you haven't. You picture yourself asking when you meet next. As you think about the question, you feel ready.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are you looking forward to asking your advisor a question?

Undergrad scenario body: You walk into a quiet lecture hall a few minutes late. The door squeaks really loudly as you enter and classmates look in your direction. They are probably feeling surprised.
Undergrad comprehension question: Are your classmates annoyed that you disturbed them?

Undergrad scenario body: An old acquaintance just moved to Charlottesville and asks you out to coffee. You are nervous about seeing them again after many years and could easily tell them that you are too busy. You are in your dorm when they call about meeting, and you decline the call.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do you take your acquaintance’s call to meet for coffee?

Undergrad scenario body: You're talking to a group of your friends at lunch. You make a joke that you think is really funny, but no one really laughs. They were probably distracted.
Undergrad comprehension question: Did you say the wrong thing?

Undergrad scenario body: You are about to audition for a play and you feel your body tremble. You don't usually feel this way when you perform in front of people. These trembling feelings mean that you are ready.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do the trembling feelings mean that you are unprepared for the audition?

Undergrad scenario body: You just started at a new job and you work with lots of new people. You hear a few of them laughing together and talking about going out to dinner, but they don't invite you along. As you think about your future with this group, you feel okay.
Undergrad comprehension question: Is it too late to befriend your new coworkers?

Undergrad scenario body: You are new to your group of friends and they want to take you to a party tonight. You let them know that you have to be up early tomorrow morning for an important test and that you can't go with them and they seem disappointed. You think that your new friends will respect your decision to put your needs first.
Undergrad comprehension question: Do your friends judge you for not going out with them?

Undergrad scenario body: In class you overhear some classmates discussing other people and hear your name mentioned. You do not hear everything they say, but when you walk by, they suddenly look away with guilt.
Undergrad scenario body #2 (negation sce: Afterwards, you realize that many people engage in class gossip from time to time and that your classmates probably did not mean to make you feel bad. In fact, it is possible that they were saying something positive.
Undergrad comprehension question: Were your classmates saying negative things about you?
